questions.question: What is the result of the expression 10 / 3 in Java?
What is the result of 10.0 / 3.0 in Java?
What happens when you execute 10 / 0 in Java?
options: The program silently ignores the error

questions.question: What is the result of 10.0 / 0.0 in Java?
What does the modulo operator (%) return?
options: The absolute value of the division

questions.question: What is the result of the expression -7 % 3 in Java?
Which expression correctly checks whether an integer n is even?
What is the result of the expression 10 / 3.0 in Java?
When used as a standalone statement (on its own line), what is the difference between x++ and ++x?
options: There is no difference in the final value of x
++x does not change the value of x
x++ decrements x instead of incrementing it
questions.answer: There is no difference in the final value of x

questions.question: What does the pre-increment operator (++x) do?
options: Uses the value of x then increments it
Increments x before the value is used in the expression
questions.answer: Increments x before the value is used in the expression

questions.question: Why does the statement b = b + 5; fail to compile when b is declared as byte?
options: byte variables cannot be used with the + operator
The result of b + 5 is promoted to int, which cannot be assigned to byte without a cast
The value 5 is too large for a byte
questions.answer: The result of b + 5 is promoted to int, which cannot be assigned to byte without a cast

questions.question: Why does b += 5; compile successfully when b is a byte variable?
options: The += operator does not perform arithmetic
The += operator skips type checking entirely
Compound assignment operators include an implicit cast to the target type
The compiler treats 5 as a byte literal
questions.answer: Compound assignment operators include an implicit cast to the target type

questions.question: What is the result of 15 % 5 in Java?
Given 250 total minutes, which expressions correctly compute hours and remaining minutes?
Which of the following is NOT a valid compound assignment operator in Java?

options: An error because += cannot be used with String

questions.question: In the expression int y = x--;, what value does y receive?
options: The value of x after decrementing
The value of x before decrementing
questions.answer: The value of x before decrementing
